summary: Completed testing of 'Vertical / Soap' project type feature. All backend and frontend tests pass. Backend correctly parses EPISODE/EP/CHAPTER markers (Tier 3 regex) and accepts project_type='vertical' with genre direction injection. Frontend displays 5 project type options including 'Vertical / Soap' with proper amber styling on selection.
passed_tests: Backend: POST /api/parse-scenes correctly splits text by EPISODE markers (EPISODE 1, EPISODE 2, EPISODE 3) - PASS
Backend: POST /api/parse-scenes correctly splits text by EP markers (EP 1, EP 2, EP 3) - PASS
Backend: POST /api/parse-scenes correctly splits text by EP. markers (EP. 1, EP. 2) - PASS
Backend: POST /api/parse-scenes correctly splits text by CHAPTER markers (CHAPTER 1, CHAPTER 2, CHAPTER 3) - PASS
Backend: INT./EXT. markers (Tier 1) correctly take priority over EPISODE markers (Tier 3) - PASS
Backend: All 5 project types (commercial, tvfilm, theatre, voiceover, vertical) accepted by API - PASS
Backend: API root endpoint working - PASS
Backend: Debug pipeline endpoint working - PASS
Frontend: Project type selector (data-testid='project-type-selector') present with 5 options - PASS
Frontend: 'Vertical / Soap' button text is 'Vertical / Soap' - PASS
Frontend: Clicking 'Vertical / Soap' applies amber styling (border-amber-500/40 bg-amber-500/10 text-amber-500) - PASS
critical_code_review_comments: server.py lines 204-218: parse_scenes_regex correctly implements 3-tier regex: Tier 1 (INT./EXT.), Tier 2 (SCENE/ACT), Tier 3 (EPISODE/EP/CHAPTER). Tier 3 only activates when Tier 1 and 2 find <2 matches.
server.py line 180: SingleSceneRequest model correctly includes project_type with 'vertical' as valid option
server.py lines 1445-1449: type_labels dict includes 'vertical' -> 'Vertical drama / Soap-style series' and genre direction injection for vertical type
UploadPage.jsx lines 678-698: Project type array includes {id: 'vertical', label: 'Vertical / Soap'} with proper data-testid and amber styling on selection
seed_data_creation: Test scripts with EPISODE/EP/CHAPTER markers for scene parsing tests
context_for_next_testing_agent: Vertical / Soap project type feature fully implemented and tested. Key implementation details: 1) parse_scenes_regex has 3 tiers - INT/EXT first, then SCENE/ACT, then EPISODE/EP/CHAPTER. Tier 3 only activates when scripts don't have standard screenplay markers. 2) When project_type='vertical', the analysis_text gets [GENRE DIRECTION: ...] block with soap-style acting instructions. 3) Frontend has 5 project type buttons with data-testid='project-type-{id}' pattern.

episode_marker_parsing.details: EPISODE 1, EPISODE 2, etc. correctly split into separate scenes when no INT/EXT markers present

ep_marker_parsing.details: EP 1, EP 2, EP. 1, EP. 2 correctly split into separate scenes

chapter_marker_parsing.details: CHAPTER 1, CHAPTER 2, etc. correctly split into separate scenes

genre_direction_injection.details: When project_type='vertical', analysis_text includes [GENRE DIRECTION: ...] block with heightened soap-style acting instructions

frontend_project_type_selector.details: 5 project type options displayed including 'Vertical / Soap' with proper data-testid attributes

vertical_button_styling.details: Clicking 'Vertical / Soap' applies amber styling (border-amber-500/40 bg-amber-500/10 text-amber-500)
